item.title: ECB Must Be ‘Vigilant’ Without Rushing Rate Hikes, Muller Says
item.description: The European Central Bank needs to stay “vigilant“ to potential inflation risks from the Iran war but mustn’t act in haste, Governing Council member Madis Muller said.

item.title: Planet Money Turned Everyday Annoyances Into an Economics Book
item.description: Mary Childs and Alex Mayassi explain the world.

item.title: Odd Lots: Planet Money Explains the World (Podcast)
item.description: There are a lot of things to be annoyed about in modern life. The high cost of food and housing and childcare. Dating apps that don’t seem to work. The fear of AI replacing you at your job. These are all common complaints and concerns, and each of them can be traced to a specific economic phenomenon or market structure issue. Once you start thinking about the world in this way, you can’t unsee it. In this episode, we speak with Planet Money co-host Mary Childs, and contributor to the podcast, Al

item.title: Australia Treasury Reversed Course on Debt Office to Call Review
item.description: Australia’s Treasury initially opted to set aside complaints about the nation’s sovereign debt manager before reversing course weeks later to order an independent review, documents released Friday under a Freedom of Information Act request showed.

item.title: Abu Dhabi’s Axight Adds to Gulf M&A Wave With Brookfield Deal
item.description: Abu Dhabi’s Axight has bought a stake in an Australian alternative asset manager from Brookfield, adding to a flurry of dealmaking by regional entities despite disruptions caused by the Gulf war.

item.title: Japan’s Wage Growth Holds Momentum After Small Firms Reach Deals
item.description: Japan’s largest labor union group reported that workers under its umbrella secured an average pay increase exceeding 5% in this year’s wage talks even after results from smaller firms were factored into the tally, an outcome that will keep the Bank of Japan on track for a near-term interest rate hike.

item.title: Trump-Linked Investors Want to Pour $1.8 Billion Into Bosnia
item.description: Two figures who worked on President Donald Trump’s attempt to overturn the results of the 2020 election are pursuing a major infrastructure project in Bosnia.

item.title: JPMorgan, UBS See Little Stock Upside Left in Europe This Year
item.description: Looming downgrades to over-optimistic earnings expectations mean European stocks will struggle to rise further this year, according to strategists surveyed by Bloomberg.

item.title: Trump Says Iran’s Concessions Pave Way for Deal to End War
item.description: President Donald Trump claimed Iran has made key concessions in negotiations with the US and that a deal to end the war — which has left thousands dead and rattled energy markets — could be announced “fairly soon.”

item.title: Flexible London Landlord Workspace Warns on Profit, Dividend
item.description: Workspace Group Plc shares tumbled after the company warned investors it faces a “substantial” profit squeeze due to higher costs and lower rents that will force the London-based flexible office landlord to cut its dividend.

item.title: Australia to Sign Multibillion-Dollar Deal for Japanese Warships
item.description: Australia is set to sign a multibillion-dollar contract to buy advanced naval frigates from Japan, sealing a deal that will help modernize its navy, boost Tokyo’s ambitions of becoming a major player in the global defense market and deepen security ties between the two countries.

item.title: Bouygues-Led Group in Talks to Buy SFR in €20.4 Billion Deal
item.description: A consortium including Bouygues Telecom, Iliad SA and Orange SA has entered exclusive negotiations to buy billionaire Patrick Drahi’s telecom company SFR.

item.title: Uber Raises Delivery Hero Stake in €270 Million Prosus Deal
item.description: Uber Technologies Inc. is raising its holding in Delivery Hero SE, buying a stake from its European rival’s biggest shareholder for €270 million ($318 million).

item.title: Kenya Seeks Emergency World Bank Funds to Absorb War Impact
item.description: Kenya is in talks with the World Bank for emergency funding, among the first to go public with plans to cushion the Iran war’s impact.

item.title: FTSE 100 Set to Rise, Pound Falls as Iran Talks in Focus
Europe’s Best-Performing Stock of 2026 Rides AI Photonics Wave
item.description: A clamor by investors for stocks that stand to benefit from the boom in artificial intelligence has propelled a little‑known French company to the top of Europe’s 2026 equity rankings.

item.title: Pakistan Oil Tanker Makes Rare Entry and Exit Through Hormuz
item.description: A Pakistan-flagged tanker that entered the Persian Gulf over the weekend has become the first carrier to exit through the Strait of Hormuz with a crude cargo since a US blockade began on Monday, underscoring just how limited traffic through the vital chokepoint remains.

item.title: Wheat Rallies on Weather Woes and Iran-Linked Fertilizer Crunch
item.description: Wheat was set for its biggest weekly gain in almost two months, as persistent weather concerns and tighter fertilizer supplies linked to the Iran war stoked worries over the crop’s supply outlook.

item.title: Chipmaker Tops Moutai for Highest Price Tag Among China Stocks
item.description: A Chinese laser chipmaker surpassed Kweichow Moutai Co. to become the mainland’s highest-priced stock, highlighting a shift in investor appetite toward advanced technology and away from old economy leaders.

item.title: Japan Has a New Name for Scorching Hot Days
item.description: Japan’s weather agency has formally adopted a name for sizzling-hot days ahead of what is set to be another scorcher of a summer.

item.title: India’s Rupee Is Asia’s Biggest Gainer on Oil FX Window Report
item.description: The Indian rupee led gains in Asia after a report that the central bank has asked state-owned oil refiners to route their dollar purchases through a special credit facility.

item.title: Malaysia’s IAQ Group Is Said to Consider 1 Billion Ringgit IPO
item.description: IAQ Group is considering an initial public offering in Kuala Lumpur to raise about 1 billion ringgit ($253 million), according to people with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: ECB to Hike Rates in June as 2026 Inflation Jumps, Survey Shows
item.description: The European Central Bank will raise interest rates in June as the Iran war drives inflation higher this year, a Bloomberg survey showed.

item.title: Apple Marketing Chief for Watch, AirPods, Home and Health Retires
item.description: Apple Inc.’s marketing executive in charge of the Apple Watch, AirPods, health and smart home initiatives said he’s retiring, marking a changing of the guard for a series of key product lines.

item.title: Malaysia’s GDP Grows 5.3% as War Starts to Strain Economy
item.description: Malaysia’s economy expanded at a slower-than-expected pace in the first quarter as the Middle East conflict began to weigh on the nation’s major industries, including manufacturing and services.

item.title: China’s Surging Tech Stock Index Faces Rising Concentration Risk
item.description: China’s tech-heavy ChiNext Index is becoming increasingly concentrated as it powers to an 11-year high, buoyed by earnings beats from a small group of heavyweight stocks.

item.title: China Moves to Slow Yuan Rally by Cooling Fixing Volatility
item.description: China’s central bank is using its daily reference rate to temper the yuan’s rally as the currency’s outperformance during the Iran war fuels further bullish sentiment.

item.title: Oaktree’s Senior Credit Executive for Asia Set to Depart Firm
item.description: One of Oaktree Capital Group LLC’s most senior investment professionals for Asia is set to leave the firm in the coming months, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Foreign Trading of Chinese Bonds via Hong Kong Hits Record High
item.description: Overseas funds’ trading of Chinese onshore bonds via Hong Kong hit a record last month, underscoring growing interest for yuan assets amid escalating Middle East tensions.

item.title: China to Commission Seven Nuclear Reactors in 2026, CCTV Says
item.description: Seven nuclear power reactors are scheduled to be completed and commissioned in China this year, state broadcaster China Central Television reported, citing the nation’s atomic energy organization’s annual report.
